RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rheem Manufacturing Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant's failure to support its motion with any evidence precludes defendant from successfully sustaining its burden. (See CRC 3.1350(c).) The Court will not consider defendant's request in its reply brief to continue the hearing, as defendant already made such request to the Court in an ex parte application, which the Court denied.
